# Andrew Carnegie: life of the week

[HistoryExtra podcast](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/historyextra-podcast/id256580326)

Play

How did a man who crushed unions in Gilded Age America come to see himself as humanity’s benefactor? Speaking to Elinor Evans, historian and biographer David Nasaw explores the many contradictions of 19th-century industrialist Andrew Carnegie’s life. From his ruthless business tactics and controversial role in the violent 1892 Homestead Strike, to his reinvention as a pioneering philanthropist and self-declared enemy of war, they uncover how Carnegie shaped the age of steel – and struggled to reconcile capitalism with conscience.

The HistoryExtra podcast is produced by the team behind BBC History Magazine.
